summary: In patients requiring retreating due to an apical infection, Enterococcus faecalis frequently causes these issues because it resists standard treatment methods and forms durable biofilms inside tooth roots. This makes E. faecalis the predominant microorganism identified in repeated instances of periapical disease despite previous interventions.
